key_insight: SciVerseGym turns crystal discovery into a Gymnasium-style sequential decision problem, letting agents edit structures and get evaluator feedback in a closed loop.
engineering_relevance: It gives materials researchers a reusable, modular testbed for reinforcement learning, Bayesian optimization, evolutionary search, and agent workflows without rebuilding the crystal-search pipeline each time.
method: Supports local and global crystal edits: substitution, lattice perturbation, atomic displacement, vacancy creation, and atom insertion.
Works with configurable observations, rewards, chemical spaces, and optional relaxation using machine-learned interatomic potentials or any ASE-compatible calculator.
Returns standard RL tuples (obs, reward, terminated, truncated, info) to integrate with existing agent frameworks.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a framework-level evaluation paradigm rather than a dataset benchmark, using candidate structures scored by ML interatomic potentials or ASE-compatible calculators with optional stability and phonon diagnostics.
watch_item: Performance and scientific validity will depend on the quality of the chosen potential, relaxation settings, and reward design, which may bias search outcomes.
paper_title: SVGym (SciVerseGym): An Environment for Reinforcement Learning and Bayesian Optimization in Crystal Discovery
